summary: Native checkbox family with one raw Checkbox atom, two field-level molecules, and shared React/Vue contracts for atom and grouped multi-select behavior.
philosophy: Teach CheckboxField and CheckboxGroupField as the canonical labeled paths, and keep raw Checkbox as the low-level escape hatch.
Keep native input and fieldset semantics as the real contract instead of inventing Marwes metadata where standard HTML already expresses the meaning honestly.
Treat indeterminate as an explicit controlled-state affordance for select-all flows, not as a separate durable selection state.
Keep single-field and grouped-field accessibility wiring source-owned in shared field helpers so React and Vue stay aligned.

useWhen: The UI needs one or more native checkbox controls with optional description or error support.
The product wants the canonical labeled path for a single checkbox or a multi-select checkbox group without rebuilding the field wiring manually.
The team wants React and Vue checkbox behavior to stay aligned through one core recipe, shared field helpers, and shared contract files.
avoidWhen: A switch, radio group, or always-on content toggle would be the more honest interaction model.
The layout only needs a decorative checkmark treatment and does not represent a real form control.
The team expects a purpose-wrapper or semantic-metadata layer that the current Checkbox family does not ship.

automationCovers: Core recipe tests for controlled versus uncontrolled state output on the raw checkbox atom.
Shared checkbox and checkbox-field contracts across React and Vue for checked state, single-field naming, description/error wiring, invalid state, and disabled forwarding.
Shared checkbox-group-field contracts across React and Vue for group labeling, description wiring, error wiring, invalid propagation, and indeterminate support.
Storybook introduction and taxonomy tests in both apps.
manualReviewRequired: Product-level judgment about when a select-all checkbox should become indeterminate and whether that behavior stays truthful in real flows.
Real browser review for focus visibility and appearance consistency because the family resets native checkbox appearance heavily in CSS.
Whether label, helper text, and error wording stay clear when groups get long or validation messages become more complex.
notes: Checkbox completed a first-pass accessibility audit focused on single-field contract maturity and native-first field wiring.
The family now has shared contracts for the raw atom, single-field molecule, and grouped molecule across React and Vue.

openQuestions: Which Checkbox-family stories should join the first automated accessibility smoke set?
Should the local Figma sync gain clearer molecule-level CheckboxField and CheckboxGroupField refs that mirror the shipped helper-text and error behavior more directly?
